title: Total volume of a rectangular prism and cylinder
problem: A trophy base consists of a rectangular prism (length $12$ cm, width $10$ cm, height $3$ cm) with a cylinder (radius $3$ cm, height $8$ cm) on top. Find the total volume, correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Find the volume of the rectangular prism.
Find the volume of the cylinder.
Add the two volumes to find the total.

title: Volume of a solid with a cylindrical hole removed
problem: A metal block has dimensions $24$ cm by $16$ cm by $10$ cm. A cylindrical hole of radius $3$ cm is drilled completely through it (depth $10$ cm). Find the remaining volume, correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Find the volume of the full rectangular block.
Find the volume of the cylindrical hole.
Subtract the hole volume from the block volume.

title: Volume of metal in a hollow cylindrical pipe
problem: A hollow cylindrical pipe has an outer radius of $6$ cm and an inner radius of $4$ cm. The pipe is $80$ cm long. Find the volume of metal in the pipe, correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Find the area of the annular (ring-shaped) cross-section by subtracting the inner circle area from the outer circle area.
Multiply the cross-sectional area by the length of the pipe.
